sections.paragraph:
Κλήμης, εντος, ὁ Clement (the Gk. form of this Lat. name [Clemens] is found e.g. Philostrat., Vi. Soph. 2, 27, 2; Jos., Ant. 19, 37–47; OGI 207, 1; 574, 9; POxy 241, 1; 340; Sb 4613; 8089, 1 [beg. II a.d.]).

① a member of the church at Philippi, honored by Paul w. the title ‘co-worker’ (a Clement of Philippi is mentioned CIL III 633) Phil 4:3.

② a member of the church at Rome, in charge of relations w. other churches Hv 2, 4, 3, sometimes Identified w. 1, though without sufficient reason. The pers. meant is certainly the author of 1 Cl; he is named in the subscr. of that letter; also subscr. of 2 Cl., and tradition from the middle of the second cent. recognizes him as the third bishop of Rome.—M-M.

Gk. Gk. = Greek

Lat. Lat. = Latin

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

beg. beg. = beginning

CIL CIL = Corpus Inscriptionum Latinarum—List 3

Hv Hv = Visions

pers. pers. = person(s)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

subscr. subscr. = subscription

2 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 547
